title: gynura aurantiaca
intro: Gynura aurantiaca, also known as the fire leaves or red mint, is a tropical plant species native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Gynura aurantiaca include reducing inflammation and improving symptoms of arthritis, as well as aiding in digestion and reducing fever. The plant is commonly prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, as well as being used in capsule or tablet form, and is also sometimes used in traditional cooking as a spice or garnish.
